story.iWant: concurrency stress tests for TenantContext ThreadLocal management
story.soThat: race conditions and memory model violations are detected
story.tasks: - AC1: LitmusKt dependency added (version TBD, JetBrains Research) - AC2: Concurrency test - TenantContextIsolationTest.kt (validates no cross-thread context leakage) - AC2: Concurrency test - EventProcessorPropagationTest.kt (validates async propagation correctness) - AC2: Concurrency test - ConnectionPoolContextTest.kt (validates context with pooled connections) - AC3: Tests run with multiple thread scenarios (2, 4, 8, 16 threads) - AC4: Memory model violations detected and prevented - AC5: Tests integrated into nightly CI/CD - AC6: All concurrency tests pass without race conditions - AC7: LitmusKt testing documented

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. LitmusKt dependency added (version TBD, JetBrains Research) 2. Concurrency tests in litmusTest/kotlin/ source set: - TenantContextIsolationTest.kt (validates no cross-thread context leakage) - EventProcessorPropagationTest.kt (validates async propagation correctness) - ConnectionPoolContextTest.kt (validates context with pooled connections) 3. Tests run with multiple thread scenarios (2, 4, 8, 16 threads) 4. Memory model violations detected and prevented 5. Tests integrated into nightly CI/CD 6. All concurrency tests pass without race conditions 7. LitmusKt testing documented

doc.relevance: LitmusKt framework for systematic concurrency testing on Kotlin/JVM
doc.snippet: ADR-008: LitmusKt Concurrency Testing - LitmusKt provides systematic concurrency testing for Kotlin/JVM - Detects race conditions, memory model violations, atomicity issues - Tests ThreadLocal context across multiple thread scenarios (2, 4, 8, 16 threads) - Validates no cross-thread context leakage - Tests async event processor propagation correctness - Integrated into nightly CI/CD (not fast CI - long execution time) - JetBrains Research project for Kotlin concurrency validation

LitmusKt Concurrency Testing (Layer 7 of 7-layer testing defense): - Source set: litmusTest/kotlin/ - Execution: Nightly CI/CD only (not fast feedback loop) - Coverage: ThreadLocal management, async propagation, connection pooling - Memory model: Validates Java Memory Model (JMM) correctness - Thread scenarios: 2, 4, 8, 16 threads for stress testing

doc.relevance: Business requirements for concurrency control and data consistency
doc.snippet: FR014: Data consistency with concurrency control. Multi-tenant systems require thread-safe context management. Race conditions in tenant context → cross-tenant data leaks (catastrophic). Systematic concurrency testing prevents production incidents.

doc.relevance: Epic 3 retrospective identified need for early LitmusKt setup
doc.snippet: Story 4.0 Task: Setup LitmusKt Framework Early (Dana + Charlie, ~3h) - Extract from Story 4.10 scope - Add to framework/multi-tenancy test dependencies - Create example concurrency test for TenantContext - Enable concurrency testing from Story 4.1 (not Story 4.10) Rationale: Early setup enables concurrency validation throughout Epic 4.

Story 4.10: LitmusKt Concurrency Testing for TenantContext - LitmusKt dependency added (JetBrains Research) - Concurrency tests: TenantContextIsolationTest, EventProcessorPropagationTest, ConnectionPoolContextTest - Tests run with multiple thread scenarios (2, 4, 8, 16 threads) - Memory model violations detected and prevented - Tests integrated into nightly CI/CD

artifact.reason: Story 4.1 TenantContext ThreadLocal implementation to be validated for concurrency
Story 4.5 async event processor propagation to be validated for race conditions
Existing thread safety integration test to be enhanced with LitmusKt

constraints.constraint: LitmusKt dependency from JetBrains Research (version TBD - verify availability). Repository: Maven Central or JetBrains Space If LitmusKt unavailable: Use alternative concurrency testing (Lincheck, JCStress) Document version and availability in gradle/libs.versions.toml.

Dedicated source set: litmusTest/kotlin/ Build configuration: id("eaf.litmus-testing") convention plugin Execution: Nightly CI/CD ONLY (not fast CI - long execution time ~30-60 min) Isolation: LitmusKt tests run separately from unit/integration tests.

Test with multiple thread scenarios: 2, 4, 8, 16 threads. Validates context isolation under various concurrency levels. Higher thread counts increase probability of detecting race conditions. Execution time increases with thread count (balance coverage vs performance).

LitmusKt validates Java Memory Model (JMM) correctness. Detects: race conditions, atomicity violations, visibility issues, ordering violations. ThreadLocal must be thread-safe with no cross-thread leakage. Async propagation must correctly restore context without data races.

Integrate LitmusKt tests into nightly CI/CD pipeline (NOT fast CI). Execution: After unit, integration, property, fuzz tests (~2.5h total nightly). Failure: Critical alert (indicates concurrency bug in multi-tenancy). Fast feedback loop uses unit/integration tests; nightly validates concurrency.

interface.name: TenantContextIsolationTest
interface.description: LitmusKt test validates no cross-thread context leakage. Multiple threads with different tenant contexts must remain isolated.

interface.name: EventProcessorPropagationTest
interface.description: LitmusKt test validates async event processor propagation correctness. Event metadata → ThreadLocal restoration must be race-free.

interface.name: ConnectionPoolContextTest
interface.description: LitmusKt test validates tenant context with pooled database connections. Connection reuse must not leak tenant context between requests.

standards.standard: LitmusKt framework for systematic concurrency testing. Tests run with multiple thread scenarios (2, 4, 8, 16 threads). Memory model violations detected and reported.

Coverage areas: - ThreadLocal isolation (no cross-thread leakage) - Async event processor propagation (metadata → ThreadLocal) - Connection pool context isolation (pooled connections) - Stack-based context (nested tenant scenarios) - Cleanup correctness (ThreadLocal cleared after use)
LitmusKt tests run ONLY in nightly CI/CD (not fast feedback). Execution time: ~30-60 minutes (long-running stress tests). Critical failures trigger immediate alert (concurrency bugs).
If LitmusKt unavailable: Use alternative concurrency testing. Options: Lincheck (JetBrains), JCStress (OpenJDK), custom stress tests. Document chosen alternative and rationale.

ideas.idea: Test AC1: Verify LitmusKt dependency added to gradle/libs.versions.toml
Test AC2: TenantContextIsolationTest validates no cross-thread context leakage
Test AC2: EventProcessorPropagationTest validates async propagation correctness
Test AC2: ConnectionPoolContextTest validates context with pooled connections
Test AC3: Run tests with 2, 4, 8, 16 thread scenarios
Test AC4: Memory model violations detected (atomicity, visibility, ordering)
Test AC5: Nightly CI/CD includes LitmusKt tests (not fast CI)
Test AC6: All concurrency tests pass without race conditions
Test stack-based context: Nested tenant scenarios with concurrent access
Test cleanup correctness: ThreadLocal cleared after use (no leaks)
Test stress scenario: 1000 concurrent requests with random tenant contexts
Test connection pool scenario: Reused connections do not leak tenant context
Test async event processor: Concurrent events with different tenants remain isolated
If LitmusKt unavailable: Implement alternative with Lincheck or JCStress
